programmes.show_description: Wish You Were Here: Tara visits a quaint hotel to collect her uncle after he fails to return home. But she soon realises that she and the other guests may never be allowed to leave alive.
Of Moose and Men: Hawkeye saves the life of a colonel who wants him reprimanded for disrespect. BJ tries to save the marriage of a friend who found out his wife is cheating on him.
Soldier Of The Month: A fever of unknown origin is sending men into the hospital at an alarming rate. Frank contracts the fever just as he's about to choose the 'soldier of the month'.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Way We Were: Henry makes plans to go to the club to escape what would have been his wedding anniversary. But will Matthew's plans to reunite his parents end happily ever after?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
It's A Dog's Life: Thinking Jonathan has turned into a dog, Mark embarks alone to unite a young runaway with his family.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Merry Christmas From Grandpa: On Christmas Eve, Jonathan shows the President, a farmer and an energy magnate what will happen in the future if the Earth continues to be ravaged by pollution.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Ma's Holiday: On a second honeymoon trip with Charles, Caroline can't help worrying about what's happening back home. Mr Edwards finds babysitting three active little girls is not simple.
School Mom: Caroline teaches a shy, older boy to read while she substitutes for an injured Miss Beadle. But it is ruined when mean-spirited Mrs Oleson humiliates him in front of the class.
The Raccoon: When Laura's doll breaks, Mary gives her a baby raccoon. When it bites the Ingalls' dog, Charles watches for signs of rabies, and a tearful Mary reveals Jasper also bit Laura.
The Voice of Tinker Jones: Reverend Alden's request for a new church bell starts a feud between the Kennedy's and Oleson's.
The Award: Mary is banned from taking a scholarship exam for accidentally setting fire to the barn.
The Lord is My Shepherd: Part 1: Jealous of Charles' attention for his new son, Laura longs for her Pa's affections and refuses to pray for her baby brother to get well. Part 1 of 2.
The Lord is My Shepherd: Part 2: Laura thinks she caused her baby brother's death and that her parents want a son instead. She asks God to return the baby and take her instead. Part 2 of 2.
Christmas at Plum Creek: Christmas is coming, and everyone is being secretive. Laura wants to buy something expensive for her mother but must come up with a way to pay for it.
Family Quarrel: Divorce looms when Nels storms away from Harriet after the Olesons quarrel over eggs. The situation gets more muddled when the townsfolk try to help them reconcile.
Doctor's Lady: It's love at first sight between Doc Baker and Kate Thorvald, Harriet Oleson's visiting niece. When Kate happily consents to become his wife, Doc's friends are concerned.
Plague: With an outbreak of Typhoid spreading across Walnut Grove, Charles joins forces with Doc Baker and Reverend Alden to tend to the sick and dying.
Circus Man: Jovial Willie O'Hara, a medicine salesman, comes to Walnut Grove with his 'circus'. He cures Mr Hanson's incurable headache, which gets the community talking.
Child of Pain: The Walnut Grove community intervenes after Graham Stewart is found badly beaten and unconscious on the floor of his cabin. Is he the victim of drunken rage?
Front Seat: Cold case whodunnit with Janet Suzman. Cecily retires to the seaside town of her youth, but there's no time to put her feet up as she becomes obsessed with unravelling a murder.
The Double: Mystery thriller with Camilla Power and Jason Flemyng. It's double trouble for timid Lisa when, while out with her fiancé, she meets Zoe, her confident and seductive doppelgänger.

... And Amen: Poignant finale to the classic US comedy series, starring Alan Alda. In the closing days of the Korean War, the 4077 M*A*S*H Unit throw a party in the face of irrevocable changes. (1983)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

... And Amen: Poignant finale to the classic US comedy series, starring Alan Alda. In the closing days of the Korean War, the 4077 M*A*S*H Unit throw a party in the face of irrevocable changes. (1983)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
All You Need is Love: Henry's objections towards Matthew's new girlfriend are intensified further when Enid makes a surprising discovery.
Suspect: Matthew discovers that the neighbour's son is not as perfect as everyone seems to believe. Will Henry be forced to apologise for doubting his son?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Part 1: Jonathan Smith, an angel, has been sent to help people on Earth. He befriends residents at Havencrest retirement home and the administrator Leslie Gordon.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Part 2: Jonathan is forced to reveal his real identity to Leslie's brother, Mark, in order to successfully complete his first assignment at Havencrest retirement home.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Way We Were: Henry makes plans to go to the club to escape what would have been his wedding anniversary. But will Matthew's plans to reunite his parents end happily ever after?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Night Walker: A mysterious presence moving through the night strikes fear in the hearts of the Walton's Mountain residents.
The Cloudburst: In a desperate attempt to save his newspaper from bankruptcy, John-Boy sells his meadow to a mining company. But will the company's plans for the land devastate the area?
The Great Motorcycle Race: Jim-Bob takes his first steps into the world when he enters a race, riding Ike's motorcycle.
The Pony Cart: The oldest living Walton, Aunt Martha Corrine, travels to Walton's Mountain for an extended visit.
The Best Christmas: With the Walton children growing up, will this be the last time the entire family will be together at Christmas?
The Last Mustang: Grandpa is in a determined mood when a wild mustang is captured. Will he get his wish and see the mustang roaming free on Walton's Mountain?
The Rebellion: Grandma becomes upset when she is asked to share her role as church organist.
The Ferris Wheel: Elizabeth is found sleepwalking and having recurring nightmares about falling from a Ferris wheel. Meanwhile, Ben believes his height is affecting how people treat him.
The Elopement: Erin attempts to elope with Chad Marshall, and Jason takes over the store while Ike is away.
John's Crossroad: Due to the breakdown of the mill and mounting bills, John takes an office job in Charlottesville.
Keanu Reeves stars in this sci-fi thriller as a researcher trying to shield the discovery of a new power source from the US government. Morgan Freeman co-stars. (1996)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Keanu Reeves stars in this sci-fi thriller as a researcher trying to shield the discovery of a new power source from the US government. Morgan Freeman co-stars. (1996)
Freefalling action with Charlie Sheen, Nastassja Kinski and James Gandolfini. A maverick skydiving instructor becomes entangled in a murderous conspiracy involving Soviet spies and lost gold. (1994)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Freefalling action with Charlie Sheen, Nastassja Kinski and James Gandolfini. A maverick skydiving instructor becomes entangled in a murderous conspiracy involving Soviet spies and lost gold. (1994)
White Gold: Thieves attempt to steal penicillin from the 4077th but Klinger catches one of them. Hawkeye and Trapper are suspicious when Colonel Flagg of the CIA arrives to investigate.
Abyssinia, Henry: Radar brings a message to Colonel Henry Blake; he's been discharged from the army. Everyone at the 4077th prepares to say goodbye to him as he heads for home.
